hardware: c8a.4xlarge (AMD Zen5 EPYC 9R45, 16 vCPU) on-demand, ap-northeast-1; kernel microbench cross-checked on M3 (NEON)
change: hamming() gains a fixed-width fast path for 4-word (256-bit) codes: fully unrolled XOR+popcount, no loop control. Generic loop untouched for other widths.

kernel_microbench_gcmp_per_s.note: simdbench --bits 256, single thread. baseline = generic count_ones loop.

selection_check.note: counting selection still loses to the heap at 10M (extra dists buffer traffic)

comparison.gain: +27-30% QPS at identical recall; recall bit-identical at every config

regime_note: The faster kernel re-prices the compute/bandwidth balance: 068 (slow kernel) found batch=1 optimal and tiling useless; with ~3x cheaper popcount the scan is fast enough that DRAM matters again, so tiling pays (+31% batch=1->8) and the optimum returns to batch=8. Deeper tiles (16/32) give nothing back.
